title: gastrolobium praemorsum
intro: Gastrolobium praemorsum, also known as poison, is a plant species native to Western Australia, particularly in the southwestern region. It is a shrub with toxic properties, containing glycosides, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The medicinal properties of Gastrolobium praemorsum include its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate various health conditions. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and its herbal preparations have been used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
